highlights.summary:
A long-standing and renowned online education platform from France, OpenClassrooms features more than 650 free, high-quality online courses taught by over 3000 mentors. This platform has 300,000+ monthly learners for topics including digital marketing, data analysis, and project management.

OpenClassrooms also provides around 50 job-oriented learning paths. These are accredited and paid diploma/degree programmes designed to enhance career prospects. They span six to 24 months and are available at highly affordable costs or with complete criteria-based funding, making them ideal for freshers and working professionals.

This easy-to-use and comprehensive platform aims to provide courses and training 100% online for working professionals, intending to help 500,000 individuals achieve positive career outcomes each year by 2025.

The MediaSchool group and French Tech Academy India are among the top list of growing education partners of OpenClassrooms today.

faqs.question: Is OpenClassrooms free?
faqs.answer: OpenClassrooms offers free courses with limitations.  You will not be able to obtain certificates with the free option, but there is no limit to learning. It is ideal to take the Premium Solo option that comes with a monthly 20 Euro fees. With this fees, there is no limit to certifications or learning on OpenClassrooms.

faqs.question: What is the scope of MBA?
faqs.answer:
The scope of MBA course is increasing day by day. MBA is one of the big job oriented professional programme and the most one which has been sought by the students. Once you have completed your degree, you have a great job opportunity in the industrial world. You may the manager or executive in term of designation.
The management term is most suited to the managers and that's why it is the way, where you can reach on the top of the height as soon as possible by using your leadership and working proficiency. An MBA professional always plays a vital role in his/her company and this is why he used to keep in touch with the board of directors. No need to specify the companies who recruit the MBA professionals. All sectors including government and public have various industries, companies, institutions or related organizations, come in the job prospective fields for an MBA professional.
Some job profiles are listed here:
Brand Manager
Account Manager
International Business Manager
Marketing Executive
Business Technical Consultant
All the best.

faqs.question: Is the BTech Mechanical Engineering syllabus the same for all colleges?
faqs.answer: The BTech Mechanical Engineering syllabus is not the same across all colleges, as every college committee sets a particular coursework pattern at the start of every academic year to be followed for the respective batch. The syllabus includes some core subjects, interesting electives, and lab work that gives detailed knowledge to the students about the workings of the field.

faqs.question: Which Medical fields offer the best work-life balance?
faqs.answer:
Some medical field which offe best work and life balance are shown below -
Dentistry (BDS & MDS) – Fixed clinic hours, fewer emergencies.
Pharmacy (BPharm & MPharm) – Less stress compared to hospital-based jobs.
Physiotherapy (BPT & MPT) – Flexible working hours, minimal emergency calls.
Public Health & Community Medicine – Focus on preventive healthcare rather than emergency treatments.
Clinical Research & Medical Writing – Research-based work with no direct patient responsibilities.

college_info.college_name: Chandigarh University, UP Campus Scholarship Details

paragraphs: Chandigarh University, UP Campus offers merit and need-based scholarships at undergraduate and postgraduate levels. CUCET (Chandigarh University Common Entrance Test) aims to uphold Chandigarh University’s mission of offering accessible and affordable education. The test is designed to reduce financial barriers for students, ensuring that financial limitations do not hinder talented individuals from pursuing world-class education. By offering up to 100% scholarships and financial support through the test, the university encourages academic brilliance, attracts motivated students, and fosters an inclusive environment conducive to learning and personal growth.

Chandigarh University has committed up to INR 210 crores in scholarships, INR 170 crores for the CU Punjab campus and INR 40 crores for the CU Uttar Pradesh Campus. This initiative provides a pathway to exceptional educational and career opportunities, rewarding high-performing students and granting access to diverse, competitive programs across disciplines. Additionally, there are several scholarships available for reserved category students, wards of defense personnel, and early bird admission scholarships along with teaching assistantships in the form of stipends offered at CU.
Details for all these scholarships are as follows:

1. Chandigarh University Common Entrance Test (CUCET) 2025-26: To streamline the examination process, the Chandigarh University Common Entrance Test (CUCET) has been divided into two distinct phases: CUCET Phase-1 and CUCET Phase-2. The details for scholarships offered based on CUCET are as follows:

CUCET Phase II: Percentage of Waiver

Chandigarh University Common Entrance Test (CUCET) 2025-26 provides a convenient exam experience by allowing candidates to take the test from their own homes. Aspirants can check the key requirements and exam details in this space:
Key Requirements and Exam Details:
Conclusion

In conclusion, visit the official website of the university, the Chandigarh University Common Entrance Test (CUCET) not only provides a valuable opportunity to access world-class education but also ensures that financial barriers do not hinder talented students from achieving their academic dreams. With generous scholarships up to 100% based on exam performance, CUCET paves the way for students to unlock their full potential and step into a promising future.
2. Post Graduate Scholarship for Professional Courses for SC/ST
This financial assistance (offered via UGC) is offered to 1,000 SC/ST candidates who are pursuing postgraduate courses in professional* subjects via the National Scholarship Portal.
NOTE: MA, MSc, MCom, MSW, and Mass Communication & Journalism courses are not accepted as professional courses.

3. Merit Scholarship Schemes for the Academic Session 2025-26: Students at CU can also avail merit scholarships on the basis of their scores in the national level entrance exams or last qualifying exam. The details for all these scholarships are as follows:

5. NATA/ JEE (Main)-Based Scholarships for BArch students

The university has also introduced a scholarship scheme i.e. Architecture Scholarships on the basis of NATA/ JEE Main Score for the students admitted during the academic session 2025-26. The details for this scholarship are as follows:

6. Teaching Assistant Stipend: Chandigarh University also offers an assistantship option for MSc and ME students. They get INR 5,000 and INR 8,000 per month, respectively, for a maximum of 10 months per year.
7. CGPA-Based Scholarships: 10% scholarships of the total are also given to the students who have attained 9.0 or above CGPA provided that the student is not availing any other scholarship.

8. Scholarship Scheme for wards of defence personnel: 5% seats in all courses of Chandigarh University are reserved for the wards and spouses of defence martyrs and their immediate dependent members. Additionally, defence officers in service who want to take up any course at CU with a study leave are offered 20% waiver on the semester fee. Concession details are as follows:

9. CU Sports Policy for the academic year 2025-26: Chandigarh University also offers sports scholarships to students after conducting the trial rounds for them.

10. Scholarship for Family Members of CU staff (2025-26): The wards/spouses and real brother/ sisters of CU staff (with at least one year of regular service at CU) are offered 50% concession in academic fees. It will continue till the staff member is working with Chandigarh University.
11. Early Bird Scholarship for PG students (2025-26): Students seeking admission to Master of Engineering Programs can avail early-bird scholarships as per the following conditions:
12. Other fee concessions: Apart from the above-mentioned scholarships, old students of CU, single girl child, CU teachers’ ward, orphan students and alumni of CU are also awarded scholarships.
NOTE: All eligible students can avail one scholarship at one time. In case of branch change, scholarship will be discontinued.

For renewal of scholarships at Chandigarh University, it is mandatory that the students admitted under all the above schemes shall have to maintain a minimum of 7.5 CGPA in regular semester exam in first chance without any semester drop/ failing which the concession shall be discontinued.

reviews_list.review_title: Review of MAAC, Netaji Subhash Place.

review_content.Placements: MAAC Netaji Subhash Place, Pitampura provided almost 100% placements. All our batchmates got good placements in companies like Technicolor, DNEG, Rockstar, etc. The average salary package offered was around 3.5 LPA - 4 LPA.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college had 25 PCs for practicals that were made available for students. There was a large crowd in the morning. I recommend you take the afternoon batch. You could practice very easily. PCs were of different configurations. Some were high-end. Some were for graphics software, not 3D or VFX. Classrooms could accommodate 15-20 students at a time. So, batches were not more than that.

reviews_list.batch_info: AD3DEDGE PLUS - Advanced Program in 3D Animation - Batch of 2023
reviews_list.review_title: I am not satisfied with its faculty members, and personal computers are also not good.

review_content.Placements: All students are placed in good companies by faculty members. Technicolor, MPC, DNEG, Saras Techno, and Lakshya Digital are the top recruiting companies. Our college provides jobs directly. There are few internships in this college. The top roles offered are texturing artists or animators.
review_content.Faculty: Faculty members are good before, but now they are average. Apart from 1-2 faculty members, there are no good faculty members left whose way of studying I could understand. Some explain fast, and some explain very slowly. Faculty members keep changing in this college. They charge extra for exams, and they do not tell this during admission. Taking part in top competitions is difficult, because they charge for everything starting from Rs 2,000 at least.
